(**CS**), also known by the trademark **cassingle**, or capitalised as the trademark **Cassette Single**, is a music [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") supplied in the form of a [cassette tape](/wiki/Cassette_tape "Cassette tape"). The cassette single was introduced in [1980](/wiki/1980_in_music "1980 in music").[[2]](#cite_note-Guardian-2)

A **cassette single** (**CS**), also known by the trademark **cassingle**, or capitalised as the trademark **Cassette Single**, is a music [single](/wiki/Single_(music) "Single (music)") supplied in the form of a [cassette tape](/wiki/Cassette_tape "Cassette tape"). The cassette single was introduced in [1980](/wiki/1980_in_music "1980 in music").[[2]](#cite_note-Guardian-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cassette_single&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/C%C2%B730_C%C2%B760_C%C2%B790_Go.jpg/250px-C%C2%B730_C%C2%B760_C%C2%B790_Go.jpg)](/wiki/File:C%C2%B730_C%C2%B760_C%C2%B790_Go.jpg)

The first ever cassette single to be released, "[C·30 C·60 C·90 Go](/wiki/C%C2%B730_C%C2%B760_C%C2%B790_Go "C·30 C·60 C·90 Go")" from [Bow Wow Wow](/wiki/Bow_Wow_Wow "Bow Wow Wow").

The debut single "[C·30 C·60 C·90 Go](/wiki/C%C2%B730_C%C2%B760_C%C2%B790_Go "C·30 C·60 C·90 Go")" from [Bow Wow Wow](/wiki/Bow_Wow_Wow "Bow Wow Wow") (catalogue number TCEMI 5088) was the first cassette single released worldwide, issued by [EMI](/wiki/EMI "EMI") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") in [1980](/wiki/1980_in_the_United_Kingdom "1980 in the United Kingdom").[[2]](#cite_note-Guardian-2) In the [United States of America](/wiki/United_States_of_America "United States of America"), the first cassette single was released by [A&M](/wiki/A%26M_Records "A&M Records") and [I.R.S. Records](/wiki/I.R.S._Records "I.R.S. Records") in [1982](/wiki/1982_in_music "1982 in music") with [the Go-Go's](/wiki/The_Go-Go%27s "The Go-Go's")[[1]](#cite_note-Billboard-1) "[Vacation](/wiki/Vacation_(The_Go-Go%27s_song) "Vacation (The Go-Go's song)")", which contained two songs available on both sides of the tape.[[3]](#cite_note-NYT-3)

Initially, the cassette single was supplied containing two or three versions of the primary single, sometimes also together with a [B-side](/wiki/A-side_and_B-side "A-side and B-side") song. Typically, between 4 and 20 minutes of music were available on the early cassette singles,[[3]](#cite_note-NYT-3) though later offerings would be available with five or six different versions of songs.

The British independent record label [ZTT Records](/wiki/ZTT_Records "ZTT Records") made good use of the cassette single format by [1984](/wiki/1984_in_the_United_Kingdom "1984 in the United Kingdom"), with singles by [Frankie Goes to Hollywood](/wiki/Frankie_Goes_to_Hollywood "Frankie Goes to Hollywood"), [Art of Noise](/wiki/Art_of_Noise "Art of Noise") and [Propaganda](/wiki/Propaganda_(band) "Propaganda (band)") being issued in unique versions on compact cassette.

American record companies began releasing cassette singles on a large scale in 1987, when [vinyl record](/wiki/Gramophone_record "Gramophone record") [album](/wiki/Album "Album") sales were declining in favour of cassette recordings; the cassette single was meant to replace the 7-inch 45 rpm record in a similar way.[[1]](#cite_note-Billboard-1)[[3]](#cite_note-NYT-3)

The cassette single format was used as a promotion in the 1990s, with [The Walt Disney Company](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") giving a "cassingle" to attendees of *[Hercules](/wiki/Hercules_(1997_film) "Hercules (1997 film)")* promotional events.[[1]](#cite_note-Billboard-1)

## Packaging

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cassette_single&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Packaging")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/Baby%2C_I_Love_Your_Way--Freebird_Medley_%28Free_Baby%29_by_Will_to_Power_US_retail_cassette.jpg/250px-Baby%2C_I_Love_Your_Way--Freebird_Medley_%28Free_Baby%29_by_Will_to_Power_US_retail_cassette.jpg)](/wiki/File:Baby,_I_Love_Your_Way--Freebird_Medley_(Free_Baby)_by_Will_to_Power_US_retail_cassette.jpg)

Sleeve cover for the Cassette Single, *[Baby, I Love Your Way / Freebird Medley (Free Baby)](/wiki/Baby,_I_Love_Your_Way/Freebird_Medley "Baby, I Love Your Way/Freebird Medley")* by [Will to Power](/wiki/Will_to_Power_(band) "Will to Power (band)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/c/c1/Modern_Cassette_Single_in_O-Case.png/250px-Modern_Cassette_Single_in_O-Case.png)](/wiki/File:Modern_Cassette_Single_in_O-Case.png)

A relatively modern cassette single (by [T.A.T.u.](/wiki/T.A.T.u. "T.A.T.u.")) in an O case packaging. This single is sold only in the O case and does not have an insert.

Originally, most cassette singles were released in a thick paper sleeve that slipped over the outside of the cassette, called an *O case* or an *O-card*. This was then usually [shrink wrapped](/wiki/Shrink_wrap "Shrink wrap") in cellophane plastic. Some singles contained one song on each side, much as 7-inch 45 rpm records had done, but others repeated the songs on both sides. In some markets, cassette singles generally used the same packaging as standard cassettes, a rigid plastic box with a folded paper inlay insert (known as a *J-card*).

As the cassette [maxi single](/wiki/Maxi_single "Maxi single") or [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") (EP) was released,[[1]](#cite_note-Billboard-1) more intricate packaging was incorporated that looked similar to the packaging of a regular [album](/wiki/Album "Album") cassette. These were placed in standard rigid plastic cassette cases, with a folded paper/cardstock inlay "J-card" insert. Unlike a full-length cassette album, these were generally only one two-sided inlay instead of an extended fold-out (the latter as required for song lyrics, and other additional information usually found on albums). Maxi-singles usually contained four or more versions of a single song; i.e.: typically the '7-inch single', an 'album version', a shorter 'radio edit', and occasionally a 'club edit' or 'dance mix', and a '[12-inch](/wiki/Twelve-inch_single "Twelve-inch single") extended version'.[[1]](#cite_note-Billboard-1) Other options could include an '[a cappella](/wiki/A_cappella "A cappella")' version, and / or an '[instrumental](/wiki/Instrumental "Instrumental")' version. Alternatively, some cassette singles contained differing versions of two different songs, as in a '[double A-side](/wiki/A-side_and_B-side#Double_A-side "A-side and B-side")'.[[4]](#cite_note-Whitburn-4)

## Popularity

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cassette_single&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Popularity")]

In the U.S., cassette singles were the most popular single format from 1989 to 1996, overtaking vinyl singles in the former year and being overtaken by [CD singles](/wiki/CD_single "CD single") in 1997.[[5]](#cite_note-5) In 1989, [Roxette](/wiki/Roxette "Roxette")'s "[Listen to Your Heart](/wiki/Listen_to_Your_Heart_(Roxette_song) "Listen to Your Heart (Roxette song)")" became the first [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") number-one single to only be commercially available as a cassette single, and many other singles released up to the mid 1990s were only available to purchase on this format in the U.S. Cassette singles were completely phased out in most countries by the mid 2000s. In October 2012, American alternative metal band [Deftones](/wiki/Deftones "Deftones") released "[Leathers](/wiki/Leathers_(Deftones_song) "Leathers (Deftones song)")," the lead single from their album *[Koi No Yokan](/wiki/Koi_No_Yokan "Koi No Yokan")*, as a cassette single. In April 2013, [psychedelic rock](/wiki/Psychedelic_rock "Psychedelic rock") band [MGMT](/wiki/MGMT "MGMT") released the first single from their [third album](/wiki/MGMT_(album) "MGMT (album)") as a cassette single, and October 2014 saw the cassette single "Great Big Happy Green Moonface" from [Polaris](/wiki/Polaris_(American_band) "Polaris (American band)"), the band's first release in fifteen years. In 2020, [Lady Gaga](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga") released cassette singles for her songs "[Stupid Love](/wiki/Stupid_Love_(Lady_Gaga_song) "Stupid Love (Lady Gaga song)")" and "[Rain on Me](/wiki/Rain_on_Me_(Lady_Gaga_and_Ariana_Grande_song) "Rain on Me (Lady Gaga and Ariana Grande song)")" from the album *[Chromatica](/wiki/Chromatica "Chromatica")* becoming her first single releases on the format.
